examples: Run token counting with model claude-fable-5 before changing cost budgets.
Test fallback chains because a refused request can rerun on another model with different billing.
detail.new_accounting: Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, no-output refusals are not billed, fallback retries are billed at fallback-model rates, and the model is not available under zero data retention.
detail.old_accounting: Pre-Fable Anthropic routes may have used older tokenizer assumptions, no Fable-specific refusal category, and existing retention eligibility rules.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official release-note statements about tokenizer and fallback billing
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: token counts can increase versus models before Claude Opus 4.7; fallback requests are billed at the fallback model rates

impacts.recommended_action: Re-run token counting and cost forecasts before routing Fable 5 traffic, especially for long-context workloads and fallback chains.
Keep zero-retention workloads off Fable 5 until the provider documents support for those accounts or an approved exception exists.

limitations: APW records provider-stated accounting semantics, not measured invoice deltas.
No raw release-note body is copied into the event.
summary: Anthropic's June 9 Claude Platform release notes say Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, adds refusal and fallback handling semantics, and requires 30-day data retention on the Claude API.
title: Anthropic Listed Fable 5 Tokenizer, Fallback Billing, And Retention Constraints

examples: Use tools[].max_tokens to cap tool output on short-review workflows.
Classify no-output refusal responses separately from billed completion responses.
detail.new_accounting: Tool definitions can cap output with max_tokens, and Claude API requests that return stop_reason=refusal before output generation are not billed.
detail.old_accounting: Tool calls could produce full-length outputs unless constrained elsewhere; refusal billing needed local invoice verification.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official release-note statements about tool max_tokens and no-output refusal billing
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: bounded tool output and no billing for refusals before output generation

impacts.recommended_action: Set tool max_tokens for workloads that do not need full-length tool responses and update billing attribution for no-output refusals.
Expose tool max_tokens in API wrappers and make refusal billing logic depend on whether output tokens were generated.

limitations: APW records provider-stated billing behavior and does not reconcile invoices.
No raw release-note text is committed.
summary: Anthropic's June 2 Claude Platform release notes list a tool-level max_tokens cap for output control and state that no-output refusal responses on the Claude API are no longer billed.
title: Anthropic Added Tool max_tokens And No-Output Refusal Billing Relief

detail.new_accounting: 24-hour prompt cache retention default for organizations without zero-data-retention enabled
detail.old_accounting: in-memory prompt cache retention default for organizations without zero-data-retention enabled

evidence_refs.license_note: Official changelog reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.basis: official OpenAI API changelog entry dated 2026-05-29
estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: affected workloads may receive more extended prompt-cache hits by default

impacts.recommended_action: Review prompt-cache assumptions, privacy controls, and cost forecasts for OpenAI API workloads in organizations without zero-data-retention enabled.
Confirm organization data-retention settings and explicit prompt-cache controls before relying on previous in-memory-only cache behavior.

limitations: APW records the public default-change fact only; exact cache pricing, privacy controls, and opt-out behavior should be verified against current OpenAI API and organization settings documentation.
summary: OpenAI changed the default prompt cache retention behavior for organizations without zero-data-retention enabled, making extended prompt caching the default path for affected API calls.
title: OpenAI Changed Prompt Cache Retention Defaults For Non-ZDR Organizations
